route.name: The Wydaho One Hundred
route.description: Public GPX mountain overnighter with dirt roads, trail/forest connectors, and high-country exposure.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX mountain overnighter with dirt roads, trail/forest connectors, and high-country exposure.
route.bestSeason: Summer after snowmelt; July can work after weather, water, and camp checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX mountain overnighter with dirt roads, trail/forest connectors, and high-country exposure.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Mountain water sources need final spacing/treatment checks.
resources.food: Carry route food unless service stops are confirmed.
resources.camping: Legal camp options must be selected and verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
No exact legal July 11 overnight site or lodging is confirmed; dispersed camping has legality conditions but no reservable availability proof.
Private bike-in lodging/camping requires dated confirmation and has no public availability proof.
Route roads and jurisdiction boundaries need official overlay or ranger confirmation before promotion.
Bear-safe food storage is mandatory in practice and must be documented for the chosen camp.
Water and resupply rely on route-guide waypoints and Victor business hours that need final confirmation.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Obtain Teton Basin Ranger District confirmation for route roads, dispersed-camp legality, food-storage order applicability, and any closures.
Select exact July 11 overnight coordinates or lodging and document legal status, availability, water/trailhead setbacks, and a backup.
Overlay the GPX against Grand Teton National Park and Bridger-Teton boundaries and rules.
Refresh Caribou-Targhee, Bridger-Teton, Grand Teton, Idaho 511, AirNow, InciWeb/NIFC, and NWS forecasts within 48 hours and morning-of.
Confirm McReynolds Reservoir, Teton River/Harrops Bridge, Packsaddle Creek, South Leigh Creek, and Victor service hours.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Mountain route can fit a strong two-day July effort after storms, snow, water, and camp checks.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
